text: I used to build van de graffs and i found that metalic painted paper works great and dosnt damage the rubber band.

author: @balbin23
text:
I have finnaly finished my machine and even on 1/5 motor power sparks are 20-30 cm long !
I could not make a toroid but  without it machine is working perfect ..Combs are excatly in positions as you have shown.They are  made on cooper board with small nails thru and soldered.Terminal is made from two large about 35 cm salad bowls from IKEA..Just one question it is save to power VDG in my room full of computers wi fi and stuff?
 soon I will make short clip with my construction Cheerrs brother .

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Oh no - that's a really bad idea. A VDGG of this power will discharge through the house electrics and fry anything that is plugged in. The best idea is to sink a copper pipe into the ground outside and attach an earth cable then run it to the VDGG.

author: @balbin23
text: Thanks for respons..I'm kind of green with electricity and stuff so what are you suggesting how my suppose to connect lower comb ? I was thinking  of  using ground terminal from electricity socket is that a good idea?

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Certainly, but it's is without a doubt going to be better if you sink a dedicated earth

author: @balbin23
text:
Hello 
I'm almost finished with my VDG . Just one question:can you connect lower combs to ground via heating or water pipes in home ?
